compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin user, I am notified at session start when my installed artifacts are stale
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A SessionStart hook (`check-artifact-versions.sh`) that detects stale or legacy DSO artifacts in a host project and prints a single notification directing the user to run `dso update-artifacts`. A 24-hour repo-scoped cache prevents noise on every session open.

**Why**: Without a session-start notification, host project developers have no way to know their artifacts are stale without manually inspecting them. This surface requires no active user intent — it surfaces staleness automatically at the start of every working session.

**Scope**:
- IN: `check-artifact-versions.sh` reads version stamps from all 4 installed artifacts and compares against current plugin version from `plugin.json`
- IN: 24-hour repo-scoped cache at `.claude/dso-artifact-check-cache`; stores plugin version string AND timestamp; valid only when both match
- IN: SessionStart hook registration in `plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`
- IN: Silent exit when running inside the plugin source repo (sentinel: `plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` present in CWD)
- IN: Legacy artifact detection (absent stamp → "legacy/unknown" designation with one-time migration notice)
- OUT: Actually applying updates — that is S3's scope
- OUT: Adding `.claude/dso-artifact-check-cache` to `.gitignore` — that is S1's scope (via `dso-setup.sh`)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `check-artifact-versions.sh` reads the version stamp from each of the 4 installed artifacts in the host project CWD and compares against the current plugin version from `plugin.json`; when any artifact is stale or has no stamp (legacy), a single human-readable notification lists which artifacts need updating and instructs the user to run `dso update-artifacts`; when all stamps match the current plugin version, the hook exits silently
  ← Satisfies: "Users are notified at session start when installed artifacts are stale"
- When this story is complete, the cache file (`.claude/dso-artifact-check-cache`) stores the plugin version string from `plugin.json` AND the check timestamp; the cache is valid only when the stored version matches the current `plugin.json` version AND the cache age is less than 24 hours; when either condition fails, all 4 artifacts are re-checked
  ← Satisfies: "24h cache prevents notification noise without masking plugin version changes"
- When this story is complete, the hook exits silently when `plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` is present in the current working directory, detecting that the hook is running inside the plugin source repo rather than a host project
  ← Satisfies: "Hook does not produce spurious notifications in plugin development sessions"
- When this story is complete, the hook is registered as a SessionStart hook in `plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`
  ← Satisfies: "Users are notified at session start when installed artifacts are stale"
- When this story is complete, `tests/scripts/test-artifact-versions.sh` covers: all-current (silent exit), one-stale (notification), all-stale (notification), legacy artifact (notification with migration notice), cache-hit with matching version and age < 24h (no artifact files read), cache-bust on version change within 24h (re-check fires), plugin-source-repo context (silent exit), and verifies `.claude/dso-artifact-check-cache` is listed in the host project `.gitignore`
  ← Satisfies: "Session-start notification is reliable and low-noise"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Cache must store plugin version string (not just timestamp) — time-only invalidation silently fails to detect plugin version changes within the 24h window
- [Reliability] Hook must be fail-open: if `plugin.json` is unreadable or stamp parsing fails, exit silently rather than blocking session start
- [Testing] Cache-hit test must verify no artifact file reads occur; use an isolated temp directory and reset cache between test cases to prevent cross-test contamination

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Fixed: All 6 done definitions verified by completion-verifier (after remediation). check-artifact-versions.sh implemented, 23/23 tests pass.
comments.author: Test
